text: Useful work has an activation threshold rather than rising linearly from zero budget.
operationalization: Measure tokens/time to first meaningful edit, first verification, first retained patch, and compare across runs and task classes.
caveats: Single-run evidence cannot establish the full curve shape.

text: Flat-history reread imposes a major token tax on agent execution.
operationalization: Count repeated file reads without intervening edits, cache-read ratio, prompt growth, and rediscovery after compaction.
caveats: Repeated reads can be strategic rather than purely wasteful.

source: /Users/kevinlin/Downloads/TokenMartCC/docs/papers/2026-03-09-单任务十亿级token调度架构初论.md
text: State compression is useful but lossy; long-horizon tasks need mechanisms that preserve actionable state across compression boundaries.
operationalization: Inspect compaction counts, replacement-history shape, post-compaction rediscovery, and continuity failures.
caveats: Some losses are silent and therefore only partially observable.

source: /Users/kevinlin/Downloads/TokenMartCC/docs/papers/2026-03-09-单任务十亿级token调度架构初论.md
text: Layered state/context is preferable to forcing a single flat session to carry all task state.
operationalization: Inspect instruction channels, compaction rebuilds, resume continuity, and whether Codex preserves actionable state outside the immediate turn transcript.
caveats: A system can approximate layered state without naming it explicitly.
